heading: Interconnection
label: 251 Interconnection
notes: Amendments2020—Subsec. (e)(4). added par. (4).Pub. L. 116–1721999—Subsec. (e)(3). added par. (3).Pub. L. 106–81

Effective Date of 2020 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–172, § 3(b)134 Stat. 833Oct. 17, 2020“The amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall take effect on the date that is 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act [].”Oct. 17, 2020

Findings, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–172, § 2134 Stat. 832Oct. 17, 2020“Congress finds the following:“(1)According to the American Foundation for Suicide Prevention, on average, there are 129 suicides per day in the United States.“(2)To prevent future suicides, it is critical to transition the cumbersome, existing 10-digit National Suicide Hotline to a universal, easy-to-remember, 3-digit phone number and connect people in crisis with life-saving resources.“(3)It is essential that people in the United States have access to a 3-digit national suicide hotline across all geographic locations.“(4)The designated suicide hotline number will need to be both familiar and recognizable to all people in the United States.”

Findings, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–172, § 2134 Stat. 832Oct. 17, 2020“Congress finds the following:“(1)According to the American Foundation for Suicide Prevention, on average, there are 129 suicides per day in the United States.“(2)To prevent future suicides, it is critical to transition the cumbersome, existing 10-digit National Suicide Hotline to a universal, easy-to-remember, 3-digit phone number and connect people in crisis with life-saving resources.“(3)It is essential that people in the United States have access to a 3-digit national suicide hotline across all geographic locations.“(4)The designated suicide hotline number will need to be both familiar and recognizable to all people in the United States.”

children.heading: General duty of telecommunications carriers
children.label: a General duty of telecommunications carriers

children.text: to interconnect directly or indirectly with the facilities and equipment of other telecommunications carriers; and
not to install network features, functions, or capabilities that do not comply with the guidelines and standards established pursuant to section 255 or 256 of this title.

children.heading: Obligations of all local exchange carriers
children.label: b Obligations of all local exchange carriers

children.heading: Resale
children.label: 1 Resale
children.text: The duty not to prohibit, and not to impose unreasonable or discriminatory conditions or limitations on, the resale of its telecommunications services.

children.heading: Number portability
children.label: 2 Number portability
children.text: The duty to provide, to the extent technically feasible, number portability in accordance with requirements prescribed by the Commission.

children.heading: Dialing parity
children.label: 3 Dialing parity
children.text: The duty to provide dialing parity to competing providers of telephone exchange service and telephone toll service, and the duty to permit all such providers to have nondiscriminatory access to telephone numbers, operator services, directory assistance, and directory listing, with no unreasonable dialing delays.

children.heading: Access to rights-of-way
children.label: 4 Access to rights-of-way
children.text: The duty to afford access to the poles, ducts, conduits, and rights-of-way of such carrier to competing providers of telecommunications services on rates, terms, and conditions that are consistent with .section 224 of this title

children.heading: Reciprocal compensation
children.label: 5 Reciprocal compensation
children.text: The duty to establish reciprocal compensation arrangements for the transport and termination of telecommunications.

children.heading: Additional obligations of incumbent local exchange carriers
children.label: c Additional obligations of incumbent local exchange carriers

children.heading: Duty to negotiate
children.label: 1 Duty to negotiate
children.text: The duty to negotiate in good faith in accordance with the particular terms and conditions of agreements to fulfill the duties described in paragraphs (1) through (5) of subsection (b) and this subsection. The requesting telecommunications carrier also has the duty to negotiate in good faith the terms and conditions of such agreements.section 252 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: for the transmission and routing of telephone exchange service and exchange access;

children.label: B
children.text: at any technically feasible point within the carrier’s network;

children.label: C
children.text: that is at least equal in quality to that provided by the local exchange carrier to itself or to any subsidiary, affiliate, or any other party to which the carrier provides interconnection; and

children.label: D
children.text: on rates, terms, and conditions that are just, reasonable, and nondiscriminatory, in accordance with the terms and conditions of the agreement and the requirements of this section and .section 252 of this title

children.heading: Unbundled access
children.label: 3 Unbundled access
children.text: The duty to provide, to any requesting telecommunications carrier for the provision of a telecommunications service, nondiscriminatory access to network elements on an unbundled basis at any technically feasible point on rates, terms, and conditions that are just, reasonable, and nondiscriminatory in accordance with the terms and conditions of the agreement and the requirements of this section and . An incumbent local exchange carrier shall provide such unbundled network elements in a manner that allows requesting carriers to combine such elements in order to provide such telecommunications service.section 252 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: to offer for resale at wholesale rates any telecommunications service that the carrier provides at retail to subscribers who are not telecommunications carriers; and

children.label: B
children.text: not to prohibit, and not to impose unreasonable or discriminatory conditions or limitations on, the resale of such telecommunications service, except that a State commission may, consistent with regulations prescribed by the Commission under this section, prohibit a reseller that obtains at wholesale rates a telecommunications service that is available at retail only to a category of subscribers from offering such service to a different category of subscribers.

children.heading: Notice of changes
children.label: 5 Notice of changes
children.text: The duty to provide reasonable public notice of changes in the information necessary for the transmission and routing of services using that local exchange carrier’s facilities or networks, as well as of any other changes that would affect the interoperability of those facilities and networks.

children.heading: Collocation
children.label: 6 Collocation
children.text: The duty to provide, on rates, terms, and conditions that are just, reasonable, and nondiscriminatory, for physical collocation of equipment necessary for interconnection or access to unbundled network elements at the premises of the local exchange carrier, except that the carrier may provide for virtual collocation if the local exchange carrier demonstrates to the State commission that physical collocation is not practical for technical reasons or because of space limitations.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Within 6 months after , the Commission shall complete all actions necessary to establish regulations to implement the requirements of this section.February 8, 1996

children.label: A
children.text: access to such network elements as are proprietary in nature is necessary; and

children.label: B
children.text: the failure to provide access to such network elements would impair the ability of the telecommunications carrier seeking access to provide the services that it seeks to offer.

children.label: A
children.text: establishes access and interconnection obligations of local exchange carriers;

children.label: B
children.text: is consistent with the requirements of this section; and

children.label: C
children.text: does not substantially prevent implementation of the requirements of this section and the purposes of this part.

children.heading: Commission authority and jurisdiction
children.label: 1 Commission authority and jurisdiction
children.text: The Commission shall create or designate one or more impartial entities to administer telecommunications numbering and to make such numbers available on an equitable basis. The Commission shall have exclusive jurisdiction over those portions of the North American Numbering Plan that pertain to the United States. Nothing in this paragraph shall preclude the Commission from delegating to State commissions or other entities all or any portion of such jurisdiction.

children.heading: Costs
children.label: 2 Costs
children.text: The cost of establishing telecommunications numbering administration arrangements and number portability shall be borne by all telecommunications carriers on a competitively neutral basis as determined by the Commission.

children.heading: Universal emergency telephone number
children.label: 3 Universal emergency telephone number
children.text: The Commission and any agency or entity to which the Commission has delegated authority under this subsection shall designate 9–1–1 as the universal emergency telephone number within the United States for reporting an emergency to appropriate authorities and requesting assistance. The designation shall apply to both wireline and wireless telephone service. In making the designation, the Commission (and any such agency or entity) shall provide appropriate transition periods for areas in which 9–1–1 is not in use as an emergency telephone number on .October 26, 1999

children.heading: Universal telephone number for national suicide prevention and mental health crisis hotline system
children.label: 4 Universal telephone number for national suicide prevention and mental health crisis hotline system
children.text: 9–8–8 is designated as the universal telephone number within the United States for the purpose of the national suicide prevention and mental health crisis hotline system operating through the National Suicide Prevention Lifeline maintained by the Assistant Secretary for Mental Health and Substance Use under section 520E–3 of the Public Health Service Act () and through the Veterans Crisis Line maintained by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs under .42 U.S.C. 290bb–36csection 1720F(h) of title 38

children.heading: Exemption for certain rural telephone companies
children.label: 1 Exemption for certain rural telephone companies

children.heading: Exemption
children.label: A Exemption
children.text: Subsection (c) of this section shall not apply to a rural telephone company until (i) such company has received a bona fide request for interconnection, services, or network elements, and (ii) the State commission determines (under subparagraph (B)) that such request is not unduly economically burdensome, is technically feasible, and is consistent with (other than subsections (b)(7) and (c)(1)(D) thereof).section 254 of this title

children.heading: State termination of exemption and implementation schedule
children.label: B State termination of exemption and implementation schedule
children.text: The party making a bona fide request of a rural telephone company for interconnection, services, or network elements shall submit a notice of its request to the State commission. The State commission shall conduct an inquiry for the purpose of determining whether to terminate the exemption under subparagraph (A). Within 120 days after the State commission receives notice of the request, the State commission shall terminate the exemption if the request is not unduly economically burdensome, is technically feasible, and is consistent with (other than subsections (b)(7) and (c)(1)(D) thereof). Upon termination of the exemption, a State commission shall establish an implementation schedule for compliance with the request that is consistent in time and manner with Commission regulations.section 254 of this title

children.heading: Limitation on exemption
children.label: C Limitation on exemption
children.text: The exemption provided by this paragraph shall not apply with respect to a request under subsection (c) from a cable operator providing video programming, and seeking to provide any telecommunications service, in the area in which the rural telephone company provides video programming. The limitation contained in this subparagraph shall not apply to a rural telephone company that is providing video programming on .February 8, 1996

children.heading: Suspensions and modifications for rural carriers
children.label: 2 Suspensions and modifications for rural carriers
i
children.text: to avoid a significant adverse economic impact on users of telecommunications services generally;

children.label: ii
children.text: to avoid imposing a requirement that is unduly economically burdensome; or

children.label: iii
children.text: to avoid imposing a requirement that is technically infeasible; and

children.label: B
children.text: is consistent with the public interest, convenience, and necessity.

children.heading: Continued enforcement of exchange access and interconnection requirements
children.label: g Continued enforcement of exchange access and interconnection requirements
children.text: On and after , each local exchange carrier, to the extent that it provides wireline services, shall provide exchange access, information access, and exchange services for such access to interexchange carriers and information service providers in accordance with the same equal access and nondiscriminatory interconnection restrictions and obligations (including receipt of compensation) that apply to such carrier on the date immediately preceding , under any court order, consent decree, or regulation, order, or policy of the Commission, until such restrictions and obligations are explicitly superseded by regulations prescribed by the Commission after . During the period beginning on , and until such restrictions and obligations are so superseded, such restrictions and obligations shall be enforceable in the same manner as regulations of the Commission.February 8, 1996February 8, 1996February 8, 1996February 8, 1996

children.label: A
children.text: on , provided telephone exchange service in such area; andFebruary 8, 1996

children.label: i
children.text: on , was deemed to be a member of the exchange carrier association pursuant to section 69.601(b) of the Commission’s regulations (47 C.F.R. 69.601(b)); orFebruary 8, 1996

children.label: ii
children.text: is a person or entity that, on or after , became a successor or assign of a member described in clause (i).February 8, 1996

children.heading: Treatment of comparable carriers as incumbents
children.label: 2 Treatment of comparable carriers as incumbents
A
children.text: such carrier occupies a position in the market for telephone exchange service within an area that is comparable to the position occupied by a carrier described in paragraph (1);

children.label: B
children.text: such carrier has substantially replaced an incumbent local exchange carrier described in paragraph (1); and

children.label: C
children.text: such treatment is consistent with the public interest, convenience, and necessity and the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Savings provision
children.label: i Savings provision
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to limit or otherwise affect the Commission’s authority under .section 201 of this title
